SEARCH
Package com.orasaka.gateway.graphql

Class AiController

java.lang.Object
com.orasaka.gateway.graphql.AiController
@Controller public class AiController extends Object
GraphQL Controller that exposes the Orasaka AI capabilities as queries, mutations, and subscriptions.

Acts as the Backend-For-Frontend (BFF) orchestrator: resolves the authenticated user from the SecurityContextHolder, constructs an immutable OrasakaContext, and dispatches requests to the OrasakaAiClient facade. All operations are executed asynchronously on Java 21 Virtual Threads to prevent blocking the NIO event loop.

Browser clients must never call the AI Gateway or Ollama ports directly; all traffic must be routed through this controller (AGENTS.md §1.D — BFF Topology Directive).

See Also:
  • OrasakaAiClient
  • OrasakaContext
  • OrasakaSecurityFilter

    Constructors

    Description

  • Constructor Details

    • AiController

      public AiController(OrasakaAiClient aiClient, IdentityService identityService, ChatStreamService chatStreamService, IdentityInfrastructureProperties identityProperties, org.springframework.core.io.ResourceLoader resourceLoader)
      Constructs the controller and initializes a dedicated Virtual Thread executor for non-blocking GraphQL resolution.
      Parameters:
      aiClient - The core AI facade for dispatching chat, image, and speech requests.
      identityService - The service used to resolve user profiles and preferences.
      chatStreamService - The service orchestrating streaming chats.
      identityProperties - The properties for configuring identity services.
      resourceLoader - The resource loader for reading schema files.

  • Method Details

    • authorities

      @SchemaMapping(typeName="User", field="authorities") public List<String> authorities(User user)
      GraphQL schema mapping that resolves the User.authorities field into a list of strings.
      Parameters:
      user - The user object to resolve authorities for.
      Returns:
      A list of authority names as strings.

    • me

      @QueryMapping public CompletableFuture<User> me()
      GraphQL query that returns the profile of the currently authenticated user.

      Executed asynchronously on a Virtual Thread to remain non-blocking.

      Returns:
      A CompletableFuture resolving to the authenticated User.

    • updatePreferences

      @MutationMapping public CompletableFuture<User> updatePreferences(@Argument Map<String,Object> preferences)
      GraphQL mutation that merges new preference entries into the current user's profile.

      Executes the preference update and reloads the user record in a single Virtual Thread task, ensuring the returned User always reflects the persisted state.

      Parameters:
      preferences - A map of preference keys and values to merge (e.g., tts-voice).
      Returns:
      A CompletableFuture resolving to the updated User record.
      See Also:
      • IdentityService.updatePreferences(String, Map)

    • chat

      @MutationMapping public CompletableFuture<OrasakaChatResponse> chat(@Argument String prompt, @Argument String conversationId)
      GraphQL mutation for single-turn chat with the active AI provider.

      Constructs an immutable OrasakaContext from the authenticated user and dispatches the request to the OrasakaAiClient. The engine applies RAG, MCP context injection, and tool attachment before calling the underlying model. All processing is non-blocking via a dedicated Virtual Thread.

      Parameters:
      prompt - The user's text input.
      conversationId - The session identifier for conversation memory resolution.
      Returns:
      A CompletableFuture resolving to the OrasakaChatResponse.
      See Also:
      • OrasakaAiClient.chat(OrasakaChatRequest)
      • OrasakaMemoryResolver

    • chatStream

      @SubscriptionMapping public reactor.core.publisher.Flux<OrasakaChatResponse> chatStream(@Argument String prompt, @Argument String conversationId)
      GraphQL subscription that streams the AI response token-by-token as a reactive Flux.

      The response is piped natively and event-driven from the downstream client down to the subscription network emitter without artificial delays or sleeps.

      Parameters:
      prompt - The user's text input.
      conversationId - The session identifier for conversation memory isolation.
      Returns:
      A Flux of OrasakaChatResponse objects emitted reactively.
      See Also:
      • ChatStreamService.streamGraphQL(String, String, User)

    • register

      @MutationMapping public CompletableFuture<RegisterResult> register(@Argument String username, @Argument String email, @Argument String password, @Argument String language)
      GraphQL mutation for self-service user registration.

      This endpoint is intentionally public (no security context required). It delegates to IdentityService.register(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String) which validates uniqueness, BCrypt-encodes the password, and inserts the user with ROLE_USER. The result is a discriminated union — either a fully resolved User on success or an error string on a business-level rejection (e.g., duplicate email).

      Parameters:
      username - The desired display name.
      email - The user's email (must be unique in the system).
      password - The plaintext password — will be BCrypt-encoded server-side.
      language - The preferred UI language code (e.g., "en", "fr").
      Returns:
      A CompletableFuture resolving to a RegisterResult.

    • interceptionSchema

      @QueryMapping public CompletableFuture<String> interceptionSchema(@Argument String schemaId)
      Retrieves the JSON configuration schema mapping for a specific interception block.
      Parameters:
      schemaId - The identifier of the interception schema.
      Returns:
      A CompletableFuture resolving to the raw JSON schema content.

    • resolveInterception

      @MutationMapping public CompletableFuture<Boolean> resolveInterception(@Argument String interceptionType, @Argument String schemaId, @Argument Map<String,Object> responses)
      Resolves an active interception, merging the responses into the user preferences and clearing the block.
      Parameters:
      interceptionType - The type of interception workflow.
      schemaId - The configuration schema ID.
      responses - The input responses to save.
      Returns:
      A CompletableFuture resolving to true if resolution succeeded.
